question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getOutstandingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `order=desc` combined with a crafted `receiver.id=gte:X` boundary to cause the pagination cursor in the `next` link to skip airdrop records, reorganizing the visible transaction history for any observer of that account?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSort()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the `lastSerial == DEFAULT_SERIAL_VALUE` (0L) branch in `getSort()` by crafting a request that returns a fungible airdrop last, causing the generated `next` link to omit `serialnumber`, thereby silently skipping NFT airdrop records in subsequent paginated views and reorganizing the apparent transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: processRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user send two `receiver.id` parameters with conflicting operators (e.g., `receiver.id=gte:X&receiver.id=gte:Y`) to the `getOutstandingAirdrops()` endpoint, exploiting the `@Size(max = 2)` constraint without operator conflict validation, causing the `Bound` constructor to silently pick one lower bound and produce a mis-ordered or incomplete airdrop history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: processRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user send `receiver.id=ne:X` to `getOutstandingAirdrops()`, exploiting the fact that `RangeOperator.NE` is a valid enum value and `verifyUnsupported()` is never called in the controller, causing the DB query to exclude a specific receiver and reorganize the visible airdrop history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getOutstandingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `serialnumber=lt:1` to `getOutstandingAirdrops()`, causing `adjustUpperBound()` to decrement the upper bound to 0 and `getSort()` to enter the `lastSerial == DEFAULT_SERIAL_VALUE` branch, producing a `next` link that omits `serialnumber` and reorganizes subsequent pagination to skip NFT airdrops?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: EXTRACTOR] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request where the last airdrop in the response has `serialNumber == null`, causing `EXTRACTOR` to emit `serialnumber=0L` (the string `DEFAULT_SERIAL_NUMBER`) in the `next` link, which when followed causes `NumberRangeParameter.valueOf()` to fail or misparse, breaking pagination continuity and reorganizing the visible history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getPendingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `sender.id=gte:0&sender.id=lte:0` to `getPendingAirdrops()`, causing `hasEqualBounds()` to rewrite the range to `EQ` via `adjustLowerRange()`, changing the query semantics from a range to an equality check and reorganizing which pending airdrops are visible?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: processRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `getBounds()` priority logic in `TokenAirdropRequest` by supplying only `token.id` without `receiver.id`, causing `tokenIds` to become the primary bound and `serialNumbers` to become the secondary bound, reorganizing the sort order and pagination of the airdrop history compared to the expected primary-sort-by-receiver behavior?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getOutstandingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `receiver.id=gt:X` where X is `Long.MAX_VALUE - 1`, causing `getAdjustedLowerRangeValue()` to increment to `Long.MAX_VALUE`, and the `Bound` constructor's range validation `adjustedLower > adjustedUpper` to silently pass (since upper defaults to `Long.MAX_VALUE`), returning an empty result set that hides all outstanding airdrops?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: processRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user send `token.id=ne:X` to `getOutstandingAirdrops()`, exploiting the absence of `verifyUnsupported(RangeOperator.NE)` calls in the controller, causing the DB query to exclude a specific token from the airdrop history and reorganize the visible transaction records?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSort()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request to `getOutstandingAirdrops()` that returns an empty list, causing `getSort()` to return `Sort.by(order, primarySortField, TOKEN_ID, SERIAL_NUMBER)` (the else branch), and then use the resulting `next` link with `serialnumber` included to cause the next page to apply an unexpected serial filter that reorganizes the airdrop history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getOutstandingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `limit=1` with `order=desc` and a `receiver.id=lte:X` boundary to cause the `EXTRACTOR` to generate a `next` link that uses `receiver.id=lte:X` as an upper bound, and then manually modify the link to use `receiver.id=gte:X` instead, causing the next page to return records in ascending order and reorganizing the apparent transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: processRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `receiver.id=gte:0&receiver.id=lte:0` to `getOutstandingAirdrops()`, causing `hasEqualBounds()` to return true and `adjustLowerRange()` to rewrite both bounds to a single `EQ` operator, changing the query from a range scan to a point lookup and hiding all airdrops outside that single receiver ID?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getPendingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `sender.id` with two `lt` operators (e.g., `sender.id=lt:100&sender.id=lt:200`) to `getPendingAirdrops()`, exploiting the absence of `verifySingleOccurrence()` calls in the controller, causing the `Bound` to use the last-seen upper bound and reorganize which pending airdrops are returned?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: processRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `serialnumber=gt:X&serialnumber=lt:X` where the adjusted range is empty (lower > upper after adjustment), and since `primarySortField=false` for serialNumbers the `Bound` constructor does NOT throw, causing the service to execute a query with an impossible serial range that silently returns no NFT airdrops and reorganizes the visible history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getOutstandingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a cross-shard entity ID (e.g., `receiver.id=1.0.500`) to `getOutstandingAirdrops()`, causing `EntityIdRangeParameter.valueOf()` to encode a different numeric entity ID than the default shard/realm, and the resulting query to return airdrops from a different shard context, reorganizing the visible transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: processRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `token.id=gte:X&token.id=lte:X` (equal bounds) to `getOutstandingAirdrops()`, causing `hasEqualBounds()` to rewrite to `EQ` and `adjustUpperRange()` to propagate the EQ to the secondary bound, changing the query to a point lookup on token ID and hiding all airdrops for other tokens?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getOutstandingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `receiver.id=gte:X` without an upper bound to `getOutstandingAirdrops()`, causing `adjustUpperBound()` to return `Long.MAX_VALUE`, and the resulting unbounded query to return airdrops in an order that differs from the default (no-filter) order, effectively reorganizing the visible airdrop history for that account?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getPendingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `sender.id=gt:0` to `getPendingAirdrops()`, causing `getAdjustedLowerRangeValue()` to increment the lower bound by 1 (to 1), and the resulting query to exclude the sender with ID 0 from the pending airdrop history, reorganizing the visible records?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: processRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `AirdropRequestType.OUTSTANDING` vs `PENDING` field-swap (OUTSTANDING uses `SENDER_ACCOUNT_ID` as base and `RECEIVER_ACCOUNT_ID` as primary; PENDING uses `RECEIVER_ACCOUNT_ID` as base and `SENDER_ACCOUNT_ID` as primary) by querying both endpoints with the same account ID to reconstruct and compare airdrop histories, revealing information that reorganizes the apparent transaction flow?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSort()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request where the last airdrop has `serialNumber == 0L` (matching `DEFAULT_SERIAL_VALUE`), causing `getSort()` to omit `SERIAL_NUMBER` from the sort and generate a `next` link without `serialnumber`, which when followed skips all NFT airdrops with serial 0 and reorganizes the pagination history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: processRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `receiver.id=eq:X` (explicit EQ operator) to `getOutstandingAirdrops()`, and since `verifyEqualOrRange()` is never called in the controller, also supply `receiver.id=gte:Y` in the same request, causing the `Bound` to hold both an EQ and a range operator without rejection, producing an undefined query that reorganizes the airdrop history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getOutstandingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `limit=100` (MAX_LIMIT) with `order=desc` to `getOutstandingAirdrops()`, causing the response to return the last 100 airdrops in descending order, and then use the `next` link to paginate backwards through the history, effectively reorganizing the apparent chronological order of airdrop records for any observer?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: processRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `serialnumber=ne:X` to `getOutstandingAirdrops()`, exploiting the absence of `verifyUnsupported(RangeOperator.NE)` for the serialNumbers `Bound`, causing the DB query to exclude a specific serial number from the NFT airdrop history and reorganize the visible records?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: EXTRACTOR] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a response where `tokenAirdrop.getTokenId()` returns a value in `shard.realm.num` format in the `EXTRACTOR` map, while the original request used a plain `num` format, causing the `next` link to use a different ID format that resolves to a different entity and reorganizes the pagination cursor?
